review_summary: The transcript is sufficient for concept questions on the original GAN minimax objective and why raw minimax values are poor standalone diagnostics during training.

question_bank.question: When the generator G is fixed, what is the discriminator D trained to do in the original GAN minimax objective?
choices: Assign high scores to real samples and low scores to generated samples
Map real and fake samples to the same constant score
Predict the latent variable z from each real image
Minimize the distance between real samples and generated samples directly in pixel space
question_bank.explanation: With G fixed, D behaves like a binary classifier. It learns to classify real data as real and generator outputs as fake.

transcript_quotes: log one minus D of G of z

question_bank.question: Why is the raw minimax value V(G,D) a poor standalone diagnostic for GAN training progress?
choices: Because it depends on both the generator and discriminator, so its value does not directly track sample quality
Because it is always guaranteed to decrease smoothly during training
Because it only measures the generator and ignores the discriminator
Because it is undefined whenever the generator produces realistic images
question_bank.explanation: The GAN objective is a game between two networks, not a single loss that one model tries to reduce. A lower or higher raw value does not cleanly tell you whether samples are improving.
